---
title: "Home"
---

## Senior Biostatistician

I am a biostatistician with over five years of experience working with real-world clinical and public health data. My work focuses on translating complex observational and electronic health record data into actionable insights that support healthcare decision-making, program evaluation, and population health research.

I collaborate closely with clinicians, epidemiologists, and public health researchers to ensure that study design and statistical analyses are aligned with real-world clinical questions and operational needs.

---

## Research Focus

My work spans **HIV and infectious diseases, cardiovascular disease, neurology, and metabolic disorders**, with a focus on applying observational and longitudinal data to understand outcomes, disparities, and healthcare delivery patterns in real-world settings.

I am particularly interested in studies that connect data analysis to **clinical practice, health systems, and population-level decision-making**.

👉 [View selected projects →](projects.html)

---

## Areas of Expertise

Applied biostatistics for clinical and public health research with emphasis on real-world data and decision-oriented analysis.

- Study design for observational and electronic health record data  
- Regression modeling (GLM, GLMM)  
- Longitudinal data analysis  
- Survival analysis  
- Causal inference (IPTW, propensity score methods)  
- Comparative effectiveness research  
- Penalized regression (LASSO)  
- Multiple imputation  
- Multilevel and neighborhood-level analysis  

---

## Tools

R · SAS · Python · Quarto · Git · R Shiny · Markdown
